hook.headline: Two new libs that make shadcn feel static.

cards.label: 01 · meet
cards.title: Motion-Primitives = Framer Motion patterns, dropped in.
cards.body: It's a collection of pre-built React + Framer Motion components — text reveals, scroll-triggered transitions, hover micro-interactions. Copy-paste, no install ceremony. shadcn-compatible.

cards.label: 02 · meet
cards.title: Eldora UI = pure CSS animations, zero JS.
cards.body: Same energy, different stack: Eldora ships motion as CSS keyframes + Tailwind. No Framer dependency, no JS bundle hit. Use when you need animation but can't afford the runtime.

cards.label: 03 · how
cards.title: Both drop straight onto shadcn.
cards.body: Neither replaces shadcn — both extend it. Your buttons, dialogs, dropdowns stay shadcn. The hero text, the card hover, the section reveals — those become Motion-Primitives or Eldora.

cards.label: 04 · pick
cards.title: When to pick which.
cards.body: Need rich gesture + spring physics → Motion-Primitives. Need lightweight CSS-only animations that work without React → Eldora. Need both → use both. They don't fight.

cards.label: 05 · install
cards.title: Install in one command.
cards.body: Motion-Primitives: `npx motion-primitives@latest add text-reveal`. Eldora: pure copy-paste from the site. Both work alongside your existing shadcn registry.

take.use_if: Your landing pages feel flat and you want motion without rebuilding your component system.
take.skip_if: You're already deep in Framer Motion or GSAP with custom variants — adding another layer won't pay off.
